title: phyllanthus oxycarpus
intro: Phyllanthus oxycarpus, also known as Amla or Indian gooseberry, is a deciduous shrub native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae. The plant is known for its rich medicinal properties, particularly its high content of vitamin C, flavonoids, and tannins, which contribute to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Phyllanthus oxycarpus include its ability to improve liver function, reduce cholesterol levels, and boost the immune system, as well as its potential to treat various diseases such as diabetes, fever, and respiratory infections. Phyllanthus oxycarpus is traditionally used in herbal preparations, including extracts, capsules, and teas, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
